title: Build a Knowledge Graph
description: Extract content from multiple pages, then search and visualize the knowledge graph.

steps.annotation: Start with the Rust programming language Wikipedia page
Extract and cache the page content — it's automatically indexed

steps.content:
# Rust (programming language)

Rust is a general-purpose programming language emphasizing performance,
type safety, and concurrency. It enforces memory safety without a
traditional garbage collector.

## History
Originally designed by Graydon Hoare at Mozilla Research...

[cached: 2847 words, 14 entities extracted]

# WebAssembly

WebAssembly (Wasm) is a binary instruction format for a stack-based
virtual machine. It is designed as a portable compilation target for
programming languages, enabling deployment on the web.

[cached: 1923 words, 11 entities extracted]

# Mozilla

Mozilla is a free software community founded in 1998 by members of
Netscape. The Mozilla community produces the Firefox web browser,
Thunderbird email client, and other projects.

[cached: 2104 words, 9 entities extracted]
Three pages extracted and cached. The engine automatically identifies entities and relationships across pages.

steps.annotation: Search across all cached content using full-text search
Query the knowledge graph for the Rust entity and its connections
Visualize the entity graph radiating from Rust

steps.content: The knowledge graph connects entities across all your browsing sessions. Search, query, and visualize relationships — all running locally.
